chunks.text:
LangChain Demo Document
LangChain is an open-source framework for building applications powered by large language
models (LLMs). It provides tools for prompt management, chaining, retrieval, memory, agents, and
integrations with external data sources. Common use cases include chatbots, question-answering systems, Retrieval-Augmented
Generation (RAG), document analysis, and AI agents that interact with APIs and databases. A typical LangChain workflow involves loading documents, splitting them into chunks, generating
embeddings, storing vectors in a vector database, retrieving relevant chunks, and using an LLM to
generate answers.

LangChain RAG Example
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines information retrieval with text generation. Instead of relying solely on an LLM's training data, a RAG system retrieves relevant documents at
query time. Example Pipeline: PDF Documents → Text Chunks → Embeddings → Vector Database →
Retriever → LLM → Answer with Citations
Benefits of RAG include reduced hallucinations, access to private knowledge bases, up-to-date
information, and the ability to provide citations for generated answers.
